remember: Students will be able to recall the importance of transport and communication in the development of a country.
Students will be able to identify different modes of transport such as road, rail, water, and air.
Students will be able to remember the role of communication in economic development.
understand: Students will be able to explain the relationship between transport and economic development.
Students will be able to describe the role of transport in the growth of international trade.
Students will be able to understand the significance of communication in the modern world.
apply: Students will be able to apply their knowledge of transport and communication to real-life situations.
Students will be able to analyze the impact of transport and communication on the environment.
Students will be able to evaluate the role of transport and communication in the development of a region.
analyze: Students will be able to compare the advantages and disadvantages of different modes of transport.
Students will be able to analyze the impact of government policies on transport and communication.
Students will be able to assess the role of transport and communication in the growth of a country's economy.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT objective 1, to understand the importance of transport and communication in the development of a country.
To identify the different modes of transport and their role in economic development.
To analyze the relationship between transport, communication, and economic development.
board_exam_objectives: To test the student's knowledge of transport and communication networks in India and the world.
To evaluate the student's understanding of the role of transport and communication in economic development.
To assess the student's ability to analyze the impact of transport and communication on the environment and society.
